title: potentilla × collina subsp. johanniniana
intro: Potentilla × collina subsp. johanniniana, also known as Johann's potentilla, is a plant species that is a hybrid of Potentilla collina and Potentilla cinerea. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat skin conditions such as eczema, acne, and dermatitis, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Johann's potentilla include its potential to reduce inflammation, fight off infections, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are often used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be used to soothe and calm the skin.
